description:
## Bug

`src/fetcher/refresh_loop.cr:37-47`:

```crystal
CONCURRENCY_LIMIT     = 8
CONCURRENCY_SEMAPHORE = Channel(Nil).new(CONCURRENCY_LIMIT)
CONCURRENCY_AVAILABLE = Atomic(Int32).new(CONCURRENCY_LIMIT)
@@repair_mutex = Mutex.new(:unchecked)

CONCURRENCY_LIMIT.times { CONCURRENCY_SEMAPHORE.send(nil) }
```

The last line — `CONCURRENCY_LIMIT.times { CONCURRENCY_SEMAPHORE.send(nil) }` — runs at **module load time**, not at `RefreshLoop.start` time. The same is true for `@@repair_mutex = Mutex.new(:unchecked)` (a class variable assignment that runs when the class body is evaluated).

## Why it matters

- Any test or runtime context that `require`s `refresh_loop` (intentionally or transitively) will run this side effect. The semaphore channel is created and pre-filled, the mutex is allocated, and a "process-global" resource is occupied.
- The file's own comment at line 22-23 acknowledges: *"if `RefreshLoop.start` is called in a test context, the same semaphore is shared across all test cases. Call `.reset_semaphore` after each test to clean state."* But there is no easy way to "un-require" the file and reclaim the channel.
- For multi-process testing or for a hypothetical future where the refresh loop is started/stopped dynamically, the module-level init is a footgun.

## Fix

Wrap the initialization in a lazy singleton or an explicit `init` method called by `RefreshLoop.start`:

```crystal
class_getter semaphore : Tuple(Channel(Nil), Atomic(Int32), Mutex) do
  ch = Channel(Nil).new(CONCURRENCY_LIMIT)
  CONCURRENCY_LIMIT.times { ch.send(nil) }
  {ch, Atomic(Int32).new(CONCURRENCY_LIMIT), Mutex.new(:unchecked)}
end
```

Or use a module-level `ensure_initialized!` method called at the top of `RefreshLoop.start`.

## Acceptance

- `CONCURRENCY_SEMAPHORE` / `CONCURRENCY_AVAILABLE` / `@@repair_mutex` are no longer initialized at file-load time.
- The first call to `RefreshLoop.start` triggers the initialization exactly once.
- All existing call sites in the file continue to work without changes (semantic compatibility).
- `just nix-build` passes; existing Crystal specs pass; manual smoke test (`./bin/quickheadlines`) starts up cleanly.
title: Fix: module-level CONCURRENCY_SEMAPHORE initialized at require-time
